title: maxillaria umbratilis
intro:
Maxillaria umbratilis, also known as the "Night-Blooming Maxillaria," is a type of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique ability to bloom at night and close its flowers during the day.

Medicinally, the plant has been used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Maxillaria umbratilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity.

Herbal preparations of Maxillaria umbratilis typically involve infusing the roots and leaves in water or other solvents to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams.
